name: Sad Hill - circular from Covarrubias - Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park - La Yecla
name_original: Colina Triste — circular desde Covarrubias — Parque Natural Sabinares del Arlanza - La Yecla
region_intro: Mountain biking around Covarrubias offers diverse terrain within the Arlanza river valley, characterized by extensive wooded areas and natural parks. The region features the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park, which includes the Alto de Peña Alta, providing varied elevations and scenic backdrops. Riders can explore paths alongside the Arlanza river and through areas with Spanish Juniper, making for a varied riding experience. The landscape provides a mix of challenging climbs and more gentle routes. Best mountain bike trails around Covarrubias The most popular mountain bike trail is Sad Hill - circular from Covarrubias - Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park - La Yecla, a 32.6 miles (52.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Historic Center of Covarrubias – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Covarrubias, a difficult 32.0 miles (51.5 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain and views towards Peña de Carazo. Local mountain bikers also love the Historic Center of Covarrubias – Hortigüela loop from Covarrubias, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail leading through the area around Hortigüela, often completed in about 4 hours 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Covarrubias is defined by the Arlanza river valley, dense wooded areas, and the elevations of the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Covarrubias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Covarrubias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Covarrubias?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Covarrubias. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from gentle rides along the Arlanza river valley to challenging climbs within the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Covarrubias?
A: The trails around Covarrubias cater to various skill levels. You'll find 2 easy routes, 32 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners and experienced riders alike.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Covarrubias?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Covarrubias are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100804712/sad-hill-circular-from-covarrubias-sabinares-del-arlanza-natural-park-la-yecla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill - circular from Covarrubias - Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Park - La Yecla&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Covarrubias – View of Peña de Carazo loop from Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging circular ride with significant elevation.

Q: What kind of views or landmarks can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: Mountain bike trails around Covarrubias offer diverse scenery. You can expect views of the Arlanza river valley, extensive wooded areas with Spanish Juniper, and the bluish peaks of the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Alta (1078 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views, and historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families in Covarrubias?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations can vary, Covarrubias offers 2 easy mountain bike routes that might be suitable for families looking for less strenuous rides. These routes typically feature gentler terrain and lower elevation gains. For more detailed information, check individual route descriptions on komoot.

Q: Can I bring my dog on mountain bike trails in Covarrubias?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Covarrubias region, especially in natural areas like the Arlanza river valley and Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife. Always check local regulations or specific route details for any restrictions.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts natural attractions. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6062888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Churrión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is a notable natural monument. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Covarrubias,_Spain" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Azul&lt;/a&gt;, a natural spring, is located just 6 km from Hortigüela, an area through which some routes pass.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Covarrubias?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking in Covarrubias. The weather is typically milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations like Alto de Peña Alta.

Q: Are there options for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Covarrubias offers 6 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. These trails often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Covarrubias – Hortigüela loop from Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1300 meters of elevation gain, providing a substantial challenge.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Covarrubias?
A: The mountain biking experience in Covarrubias is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the Arlanza river valley and the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking near Covarrubias?
A: Covarrubias, being a popular destination, typically offers parking options within or on the outskirts of the village. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the starting points of individual routes on komoot, as many popular routes begin directly from Covarrubias, making village parking convenient.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails in Covarrubias?
A: While popular routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100804712/sad-hill-circular-from-covarrubias-sabinares-del-arlanza-natural-park-la-yecla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill - circular from Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; attract many riders, the region has over 40 trails. Exploring some of the less-trafficked moderate or difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Doña Sancha – Historic Center of Covarrubias loop from Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt;, might offer a more secluded experience, especially during off-peak hours or seasons.
tips: A work of cinematographic architecture built for the filming of the final scene of the film "Il buono, il brutto, il cattivo" ("The good, the bad and the ugly") in 1966. After 49 years of neglect, in 2015 they began the recovery work of the cemetery at the hands of volunteers from the Sad Hill Cultural Association.
Road that goes from the town of Retuerta in the direction of Covarrubias and is called Camino del Cid. It moves along the Río Arlanza.

Covarrubias is one of the vertices of the so-called Arlanza triangle along with Lerma and Santo Domingo de Silos. Three towns in the same region that have their own historical entities in Castilla y León. The historic center of Covarrubias is incredibly preserved with its traditional houses with wooden beams.

waypoints.name: Landscape on the Camino del Cid
